title: Has anyone here done a small meshcore

messages.author: Hugo
messages.text: Yea; I'd looked at the card type options like the SenseCAP T1000 and the WisMesh Tag. But, those are still a bit hefty for a small dog. Looking a bit further, it looks like the Loko can integrate with TTN, but that definitely seems like it has less coverage as well as isn't quite in the forefront of their docs. I suppose I might have to look at whether I could do something custom in a smaller "barrel" type enclosure or such.
messages.content_html: Yea; I'd looked at the card type options like the SenseCAP T1000 and the WisMesh Tag. But, those are still a bit hefty for a small dog. Looking a bit further, it looks like the Loko can integrate with TTN, but that definitely seems like it has less coverage as well as isn't quite in the forefront of their docs. I suppose I might have to look at whether I could do something custom in a smaller "barrel" type enclosure or such.

messages.author: Hugo
messages.text: oooh, interesting: It looks like the "Air" unit (the smaller one) on that Loko setup uses a Wio-E5 ( https://www.seeedstudio.com/blog/2024/04/01/loko-the-tiniest-gps-tracker-with-built-in-lora-e5-module-can-track-any-wild-animals-and-drones-with-seeed-fusion-pcba/) It looks like meshtastic does list the Wio-E5 as (community) supported: https://meshtastic.org/docs/hardware/devices/community-supported/seeed-studio/wio-series/seeed-wio-e5/?e5=wio-e5-mini Would meshcore support for the device theoretically be possible as it's using the wio-e5, I guess? (edited)
messages.content_html: oooh, interesting: It looks like the "Air" unit (the smaller one) on that Loko setup uses a Wio-E5 (https://www.seeedstudio.com/blog/2024/04/01/loko-the-tiniest-gps-tracker-with-built-in-lora-e5-module-can-track-any-wild-animals-and-drones-with-seeed-fusion-pcba/) It looks like meshtastic does list the Wio-E5 as (community) supported: https://meshtastic.org/docs/hardware/devices/community-supported/seeed-studio/wio-series/seeed-wio-e5/?e5=wio-e5-mini Would meshcore support for the device theoretically be possible as it's using the wio-e5, I guess? (edited)

Seeed Wio-E5 | Meshtastic
<Tabs

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: I bet, depending on what you need from it. Blindly sending out GPS location in a meshcore packet every X minutes is a trivial amount of code. I don't know how beefy the STM32 in the E5 is and whether it could behave as a real sensor that you can request things from.
messages.content_html: I bet, depending on what you need from it. Blindly sending out GPS location in a meshcore packet every X minutes is a trivial amount of code. I don't know how beefy the STM32 in the E5 is and whether it could behave as a real sensor that you can request things from.

messages.author: Hugo
messages.text: Yea, I think the focus for this use case would definitely be that: send GPS updates periodically; sleep as much as possible; prioritize battery life over anything else
messages.content_html: Yea, I think the focus for this use case would definitely be that: send GPS updates periodically; sleep as much as possible; prioritize battery life over anything else

messages.author: Habbie
messages.text: indeed. much like the lorawan way of operating would
messages.content_html: indeed. much like the lorawan way of operating would
messages.reactions_html: 1
